Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable for the Mei-Lin Zhao relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Mei-Lin Zhao"). Find recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland: Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack Energy, Status: In Progress, Origin: Houston TX, Destination: Portland OR, Move Start: 2026-04-17, Move End: 2026-04-28, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi. Special Requirements field captures the DOT Class 9 lithium-ion battery modules (~350 lbs, UN3481 packaging, 15-25C climate control), split-shipment requirement, 11-day interim storage gap April 17-28, Mei-Lin's direct contact (mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com, (713) 555-0819), and the GreenStack invoice deadline of April 25.
2. List tables in the MoveOps Operations base using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) as a fallback to confirm the Relocations table ID (tblRelocations01) if the direct lookup in step 1 needs verification.
3. Search CRM for the GreenStack Energy company record using crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack Energy"). Find company_greenstack so the engagement notes, deal, and contact entries can be pulled by company_id.
4. List CRM engagements filtered by GreenStack Energy using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: [company_greenstack]). Find engagement_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes - this is where the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge is documented as an estimate pending approval, Pacific Crest Logistics is identified as the only known DOT-compliant storage vendor, and the $1,650 interim hazmat storage line is noted as awaiting Derek Hollis approval.
5. Search CRM for the GreenStack deal using crm_search_deals (dealname: "GreenStack", company_id: company_greenstack). Find the active GreenStack deal tied to the Zhao relocation and read current deal notes for any newer approval signal.
6. Search CRM for Derek Hollis's contact record using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Derek Hollis"). Find contact_greenstack_hr: People Operations Manager at GreenStack Energy, derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com, (206) 555-0381 - confirms the primary GreenStack approver.
7. Search CRM for Mei-Lin Zhao's contact record using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Mei-Lin Zhao"). Find contact_greenstack_zhao: Senior Battery Chemist at GreenStack Energy, mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com, (713) 555-0819 - confirms her direct contact for the delivery confirmation email and fallback phone for the Linear ticket.
8. Search emails for any Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack hazmat message visible in Fatimah's mailbox using search_emails (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao", folder_name: "INBOX"), looking for any written approval of the $1,800 surcharge. May return empty if the primary Derek -> Emeka thread lives outside Fatimah's folders - in that case, absence of a recent signal in her inbox is itself the answer, and no approval has been routed through her.
9. Search emails using an alternate query using search_emails (query: "hazmat surcharge") to catch messages indexed under surcharge wording rather than employee name, covering anything Emeka may have looped Fatimah into.
10. Search emails for the most recent Greg Pallone / Swift Relocations thread on the April 28 pickup-from-storage slot using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone") to confirm Swift's crew is still locked in and hasn't quietly slipped.
11. Search emails for any Pacific Crest Logistics release paperwork thread for the April 28 release window using search_emails (query: "Pacific Crest").
12. Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: null) and client-side match on DocNumber "INV-2026-0418" to surface the GreenStack Q2 invoice (Zhao + Reyes, $9,440 total, due April 25 fiscal year-end) and capture its internal invoice Id for the next step.
13. Read the specific GreenStack Q2 invoice by internal Id using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "<Id from OE 12 - expected inv_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes>") to inspect the line items and determine whether the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge has actually been added or is still floating as a pending estimate.
14. Look up the GreenStack Energy QuickBooks customer using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: null) and client-side filter on DisplayName "GreenStack Energy" to read the current outstanding balance (expected ~$7,289) for money-friction context behind the approval hesitation.
15. Search Linear for the closest prior hazmat precedent using linear_list_issues (query: "Keiko Tanaka"). Find issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic: DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser - Greg Pallone at Swift handled the DOT placarding work, making this the closest internal benchmark for whether $1,800 is a defensible Class 9 surcharge.
16. Fetch the Keiko Tanaka DOT docs issue using linear_get_issue (id: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic") to read the resolution note and any captured surcharge precedent on the ticket body.
17. List comments on the Keiko Tanaka DOT docs issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic") to capture the full resolution history - Greg Pallone confirmed the DOT hazmat placarding certificate was signed at loading and countersigned at destination, moving the ticket from Urgent to In Progress.
18. Search Linear for any existing ticket on the Mei-Lin Zhao move or the hazmat surcharge using linear_list_issues (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao") so the new urgent blocker is not a duplicate. Expected to find issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack: DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium - which covers DOT documentation but does NOT cover the $1,800 surcharge approval, so a new blocker ticket is still warranted.
19. List comments on the existing Mei-Lin Zhao DOT docs ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack") to skim Chloe Vance's recent activity on the Zhao thread in case she has already flagged something about the surcharge.
20. List Linear teams using linear_list_teams (query: "Relocations") to locate team_relocations, so the urgent blocker ticket is created on the same team as Fatimah's other relocation tickets (NOT team_operations).
21. Look up Chloe Vance's Linear user record using linear_list_users (query: "Chloe Vance") so the urgent ticket can be assigned to her for triage.
22. Create an urgent Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "<team_relocations id from OE 20>", title: "URGENT - GreenStack hazmat surcharge approval pending - blocks April 28 Mei-Lin Zhao Portland delivery", description: covering the $1,800 DOT Class 9 hazmat transport surcharge still unapproved by GreenStack, Swift unable to roll from Pacific Crest to the new Portland lab without written sign-off from Derek Hollis, blocking the April 28 delivery, Mei-Lin's fallback contact (713) 555-0819 / mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com, and a link reference to related issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, priority: 1, assignee: "<Chloe Vance user_id from OE 21>", dueDate: "2026-04-28").
23. Create a comment on the existing Mei-Lin Zhao DOT docs ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack", body: noting that a new urgent blocker has been opened on the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge approval, still pending from Derek Hollis, blocking the April 28 Portland delivery, and linking back so the DOT docs thread stays connected).
24. Look up Greg Pallone's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone"). Find contact_vendor_swift: National Account Manager at Swift Relocations, greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, (214) 555-0933 - confirms Swift's primary contact before sending the April 28 reconfirmation email.
25. Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Find chloe.vance@moveops.com - confirms her email before sending the Pacific Crest chase-down ping.
26. Read recent history in the #operations Slack channel BEFORE posting using conversations_history (channel_id: "C006") so Fatimah does not re-raise a surcharge discussion the team has already chewed on.
27. Send an email to Derek Hollis using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com"], cc: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: "Action required: GreenStack hazmat surcharge approval for April 28 Portland delivery", content: requesting written approval of the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge before Swift is due to roll to the Portland lab on April 28, 2026, with Emeka Diallo copied so the account manager stays in the loop).
28. Send a short email to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com"], subject: "Reconfirming April 28 pickup - Mei-Lin Zhao DOT Class 9 from Pacific Crest", content: reconfirming that his crew is still locked in for the April 28 pickup-from-storage on the Mei-Lin Zhao DOT Class 9 shipment from Pacific Crest Logistics to the new Portland lab, and asking him to flag any schedule movement on his end as soon as possible).
29. Send a direct email to Chloe Vance using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], subject: "Need you to chase Pacific Crest on the April 28 Zhao release", content: asking Chloe to chase Pacific Crest Logistics directly to confirm the release paperwork is queued and ready for the April 28 Mei-Lin Zhao pickup, since Chloe owns the Pacific Crest vendor relationship, and noting that the urgent surcharge ticket and Derek Hollis email are already in flight).
30. Post a message in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: flagging that the $1,800 GreenStack hazmat surcharge approval is still pending with delivery only a couple of days out, Swift is supposed to roll from Pacific Crest to the new Portland lab on April 28, that Derek Hollis has been emailed with Emeka CC'd, that an urgent ticket has been opened for Chloe to triage, that Chloe has been asked to chase Pacific Crest on the release, and that the team may need to scramble on a reroute).
31. Send a short email to Mei-Lin Zhao using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com"], subject: "Your Portland lab delivery on April 28 - on-site handoff confirmation", content: letting her know the DOT Class 9 hazmat shipment is still tracking for delivery to the new Portland lab on April 28, 2026, with Swift Relocations handling the final leg from Pacific Crest Logistics, and asking her to confirm that she or another authorized GreenStack contact will be on-site at the Portland lab on April 28 to accept the hazmat handoff since Class 9 lithium cargo requires a named recipient present at delivery).
32. Update the Mei-Lin Zhao Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland", fields: {Special Requirements: existing text plus an April 26 update note that the $1,800 hazmat surcharge approval is still pending with GreenStack (Derek Hollis), an urgent Linear blocker has been opened on team_relocations and assigned to Chloe, Derek has been emailed for written sign-off ahead of April 28, and Swift (Greg Pallone) and Pacific Crest release are both being reconfirmed}}]) so the Relocations table mirrors the current state once the approval picture is clear.
33. Add a calendar event blocking the April 28 Portland lab delivery window on Fatimah's calendar using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Mei-Lin Zhao - Portland lab hazmat delivery (Swift pickup from Pacific Crest)", start_datetime: "2026-04-28T08:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-28T12:00:00") so she is not double-booked when Swift arrives at the dock.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable for the Mei-Lin Zhao relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Mei-Lin Zhao"). Find recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland: Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack Energy, Status: In Progress, Origin: Houston TX, Destination: Portland OR, Move Start: 2026-04-17, Move End: 2026-04-28, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi. Special Requirements field captures the DOT Class 9 lithium-ion battery modules (~350 lbs, UN3481 packaging, 15-25C climate control), split-shipment requirement, 11-day interim storage gap April 17-28, Mei-Lin's direct contact (mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com, (713) 555-0819), and the GreenStack invoice deadline of April 25.
2. List tables in the MoveOps Operations base using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) as a fallback to confirm the Relocations table ID (tblRelocations01) if the direct lookup in step 1 needs verification.
3. Search CRM for the GreenStack Energy company record using crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack Energy"). Find company_greenstack so the engagement notes, deal, and contact entries can be pulled by company_id.
4. List CRM engagements filtered by GreenStack Energy using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: [company_greenstack]). Find engagement_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes - this is where the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge is documented as an estimate pending approval, Pacific Crest Logistics is identified as the only known DOT-compliant storage vendor, and the $1,650 interim hazmat storage line is noted as awaiting Derek Hollis approval.
5. Search CRM for the GreenStack deal using crm_search_deals (dealname: "GreenStack", company_id: company_greenstack). Find the active GreenStack deal tied to the Zhao relocation and read current deal notes for any newer approval signal.
6. Search CRM for Derek Hollis's contact record using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Derek Hollis"). Find contact_greenstack_hr: People Operations Manager at GreenStack Energy, derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com, (206) 555-0381 - confirms the primary GreenStack approver.
7. Search CRM for Mei-Lin Zhao's contact record using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Mei-Lin Zhao"). Find contact_greenstack_zhao: Senior Battery Chemist at GreenStack Energy, mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com, (713) 555-0819 - confirms her direct contact for the delivery confirmation email and fallback phone for the Linear ticket.
8. Search emails for any Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack hazmat message visible in Fatimah's mailbox using search_emails (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao", folder_name: "INBOX"), looking for any written approval of the $1,800 surcharge. May return empty if the primary Derek -> Emeka thread lives outside Fatimah's folders - in that case, absence of a recent signal in her inbox is itself the answer, and no approval has been routed through her.
9. Search emails using an alternate query using search_emails (query: "hazmat surcharge") to catch messages indexed under surcharge wording rather than employee name, covering anything Emeka may have looped Fatimah into.
10. Search emails for the most recent Greg Pallone / Swift Relocations thread on the April 28 pickup-from-storage slot using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone") to confirm Swift's crew is still locked in and hasn't quietly slipped.
11. Search emails for any Pacific Crest Logistics release paperwork thread for the April 28 release window using search_emails (query: "Pacific Crest").
12. Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: null) and client-side match on DocNumber "INV-2026-0418" to surface the GreenStack Q2 invoice (Zhao + Reyes, $9,440 total, due April 25 fiscal year-end) and capture its internal invoice Id for the next step.
13. Read the specific GreenStack Q2 invoice by internal Id using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "<Id from OE 12 - expected inv_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes>") to inspect the line items and determine whether the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge has actually been added or is still floating as a pending estimate.
14. Look up the GreenStack Energy QuickBooks customer using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: null) and client-side filter on DisplayName "GreenStack Energy" to read the current outstanding balance (expected ~$7,289) for money-friction context behind the approval hesitation.
15. Search Linear for the closest prior hazmat precedent using linear_list_issues (query: "Keiko Tanaka"). 16. Fetch the Keiko Tanaka DOT docs issue using linear_get_issue (id: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic") to read the resolution note and any captured surcharge precedent on the ticket body.
17. List comments on the Keiko Tanaka DOT docs issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic") to capture the full resolution history - Greg Pallone confirmed the DOT hazmat placarding certificate was signed at loading and countersigned at destination, moving the ticket from Urgent to In Progress.

19. List comments on the existing Mei-Lin Zhao DOT docs ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack") to skim Chloe Vance's recent activity on the Zhao thread in case she has already flagged something about the surcharge.

22. Send an email to Derek Hollis using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com"], cc: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: "Action required: GreenStack hazmat surcharge approval for April 28 Portland delivery", content: requesting written approval of the $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge before Swift is due to roll to the Portland lab on April 28, 2026, with Emeka Diallo copied so the account manager stays in the loop).

Send a short email to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com"], subject: "Reconfirming April 28 pickup - Mei-Lin Zhao DOT Class 9 from Pacific Crest", content: reconfirming that his crew is still locked in for the April 28 pickup-from-storage on the Mei-Lin Zhao DOT Class 9 shipment from Pacific Crest Logistics to the new Portland lab, and asking him to flag any schedule movement on his end as soon as possible).
29. Send a direct email to Chloe Vance using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], subject: "Need you to chase Pacific Crest on the April 28 Zhao release", content: asking Chloe to chase Pacific Crest Logistics directly to confirm the release paperwork is queued and ready for the April 28 Mei-Lin Zhao pickup, since Chloe owns the Pacific Crest vendor relationship, and noting that the urgent surcharge ticket and Derek Hollis email are already in flight).
30. Post a message in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: flagging that the $1,800 GreenStack hazmat surcharge approval is still pending with delivery only a couple of days out, Swift is supposed to roll from Pacific Crest to the new Portland lab on April 28, that Derek Hollis has been emailed with Emeka CC'd, that an urgent ticket has been opened for Chloe to triage, that Chloe has been asked to chase Pacific Crest on the release, and that the team may need to scramble on a reroute).
31. Send a short email to Mei-Lin Zhao using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["mei-lin.zhao@greenstackenergy.com"], subject: "Your Portland lab delivery on April 28 - on-site handoff confirmation", content: letting her know the DOT Class 9 hazmat shipment is still tracking for delivery to the new Portland lab on April 28, 2026, with Swift Relocations handling the final leg from Pacific Crest Logistics, and asking her to confirm that she or another authorized GreenStack contact will be on-site at the Portland lab on April 28 to accept the hazmat handoff since Class 9 lithium cargo requires a named recipient present at delivery).
32. Update the Mei-Lin Zhao Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland", fields: {Special Requirements: existing text plus an April 26 update note that the $1,800 hazmat surcharge approval is still pending with GreenStack (Derek Hollis), an urgent Linear blocker has been opened on team_relocations and assigned to Chloe, Derek has been emailed for written sign-off ahead of April 28, and Swift (Greg Pallone) and Pacific Crest release are both being reconfirmed}}]) so the Relocations table mirrors the current state once the approval picture is clear.
33. Add a calendar event blocking the April 28 Portland lab delivery window on Fatimah's calendar using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Mei-Lin Zhao - Portland lab hazmat delivery (Swift pickup from Pacific Crest)", start_datetime: "2026-04-28T08:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-28T12:00:00") so she is not double-booked when Swift arrives at the dock.
